sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Joe, presents with a swollen and painful right knee after a skiing accident on Saturday.
He reports a pop in his knee and pain after the incident, which has been ongoing.
He has been taking Advil and applying ice to the area for pain relief.
He denies any prior knee injuries.
Past medical history: The patient reports no prior knee injuries.
He does not have any allergies.
He is a recreational skier.
Review of systems: The patient reports a little bit of bruising on his left shoulder, which he attributes to landing on it during the fall.
He denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: The patient is taking Advil as needed.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vitals are stable, with a temperature of 97.9, heart rate of 64, respirations of 16, blood pressure of 110/74, and oxygen saturation of 98%.
Physical exam: The patient has edema and ecchymosis around the right knee.
There is positive pain to palpation, and pain with flexion and extension.
The patient has full range of motion, but it is tight and painful.
Test Results: X-ray of the right knee shows no fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a knee contusion.
Problem list: Knee contusion; Bruised shoulder; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 400mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain relief.
I instructed the patient to rest the knee, apply ice for 20 minutes, 2-3 times a day, and elevate the knee.
I also ordered an X-ray of the right shoulder to rule out any potential injuries.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to reassess the knee and shoulder injuries.
